title: Simplify resume logic to continue the exact active transaction
summary: Implement the 0.6.0 runtime simplification that makes resume behavior continue the exact active non-terminal transaction instead of relying on heuristic candidate selection, string-shape matching, or replacement transaction reconstruction. This ticket updates resume-entry logic, active-transaction lookup, and continuation checks so canonical transaction state becomes the primary resume anchor, event history becomes the rebuild fallback, and `next_action` becomes the main machine-readable continuation guide for interrupted work.
outputs: Runtime resume logic that treats the exact active non-terminal transaction as the only canonical resumable work item
Updated active-transaction lookup rules that prefer healthy materialized transaction state and exact rebuild behavior over heuristic selection

Updated continuation behavior that uses the top-level continuation surface, with top-level `status`, `phase`, `next_action`, `terminal`, `semantic_summary`, `verify_state`, and `commit_state` as the primary machine-readable guide for interrupted work
Updated runtime checks that distinguish clearly between no active transaction, active non-terminal transaction, and terminal historical transactions
Updated bounded compatibility handling for historical legacy histories without reintroducing heuristic resume behavior into new runtime logic
Regression coverage for exact active-transaction resume behavior and interruption-safe continuation
acceptance_criteria: Resume logic continues the exact active non-terminal transaction when one exists.
Resume logic does not heuristically pick a replacement transaction candidate when canonical active transaction state is available.
Healthy materialized transaction state is treated as the primary resume entrypoint.
Event-history rebuild is used as a fallback only when materialized transaction state is missing, incomplete, or inconsistent.
Resume logic no longer depends on transaction-name prefixes, substring matching, ticket-name-derived inference, or similar identity heuristics in normal flow.

Resume logic uses the top-level continuation surface — canonical top-level `status`, `phase`, `next_action`, `terminal`, `semantic_summary`, `verify_state`, and `commit_state` — together with exact active transaction identity when `active_tx` is present, to decide continuation behavior.
If the active transaction is terminal, resume does not re-activate it as current work.
If no active non-terminal transaction exists, runtime behavior distinguishes that state clearly from interrupted active work.
If canonical persistence is malformed or ambiguous, resume fails clearly instead of silently guessing a continuation target.
Historical legacy histories remain replayable under bounded compatibility handling without weakening the strict exact-active-transaction behavior of new runtime logic.

Regression tests cover at least: healthy-state resume, rebuild-based resume, interrupted verify continuation, interrupted commit continuation, post-commit pre-end continuation, and terminal re-entry behavior.
The implementation preserves canonical lifecycle ordering, resumability, replay integrity, and explicit terminal-state handling.
tasks: Identify all runtime resume-entry code paths that currently infer, select, or recover resumable work through heuristic transaction matching or fallback selection.
Update resume-entry behavior so it first loads canonical materialized transaction state and validates whether an exact active non-terminal transaction exists.
Update fallback behavior so event-history rebuild is used only when materialized transaction state is missing, incomplete, or inconsistent.
Ensure rebuild logic reconstructs the exact active transaction from canonical history instead of guessing a replacement candidate.

Update continuation logic so the top-level continuation surface is loaded first, with top-level `status`, `phase`, `next_action`, `terminal`, `semantic_summary`, `verify_state`, and `commit_state` treated as the canonical resume surface, and with top-level `next_action` as the primary machine-readable guide for how interrupted work should proceed after resume.
Ensure resume behavior distinguishes clearly between active non-terminal work, terminal historical work, and no active work.
Ensure interrupted verify, commit, and post-commit pre-terminal states resume the same active transaction without duplicating logical completion.
Remove or refactor runtime logic that uses ticket-derived inference, transaction string-shape checks, timestamp-first selection, or other heuristic matching to decide which transaction to resume.
Update validation and error behavior so malformed or ambiguous canonical persistence fails clearly rather than silently guessing a continuation target.
Add or update regression tests covering exact active-transaction resume, rebuild fallback, and idempotent continuation across key interruption checkpoints.
Document any bounded compatibility rules required so legacy histories remain replayable without reintroducing heuristic resume selection into new runtime behavior.
notes: This ticket is runtime implementation work, not only contract-definition work.
The most important outcome is that resume behavior becomes exact and deterministic: continue the active transaction if it exists, otherwise do not invent one.
This ticket should make interruption recovery simpler by centering runtime behavior on exact active transaction state plus `next_action`.
The implementation should prefer explicit failure over hidden heuristic recovery when canonical persistence is malformed or ambiguous.
Historical compatibility matters, but it must not dilute the strict exact-active-transaction behavior expected from new runtime logic.

resume_model.primary_resume_surface: The top-level continuation surface: top-level canonical `status`, `phase`, `next_action`, `terminal`, `semantic_summary`, `verify_state`, and `commit_state`, together with exact active transaction identity when `active_tx` is present.
resume_model.fallback_resume_anchor: Canonical event-history rebuild when materialized state is missing, incomplete, or inconsistent.

continuation_rules.active_non_terminal: Resume must continue the exact active transaction and follow the top-level continuation surface, especially top-level `next_action`, while treating top-level `status`, `phase`, `terminal`, `semantic_summary`, `verify_state`, and `commit_state` as canonical resume inputs.
continuation_rules.terminal_transaction: Resume must not re-activate a terminal transaction as active work.
continuation_rules.no_active_transaction: Runtime behavior must represent and handle the absence of active non-terminal work explicitly, including the no-active state where `active_tx` is null and top-level continuation fields represent begin-ready state.

error_behavior.ambiguous_state: Fail clearly when canonical persistence is ambiguous and no safe deterministic continuation target exists.
error_behavior.malformed_state: Fail clearly when canonical persistence is malformed and must not be silently treated as resumable baseline state.
error_behavior.design_goal: Prefer explicit correctness failure over silent heuristic continuation.

compatibility_rules.historical_replay: Legacy historical data remains replayable as bounded compatibility behavior.
compatibility_rules.new_runtime_behavior: New resume logic must remain exact-active-transaction-first and must not reintroduce heuristic candidate selection.
compatibility_rules.no_forced_rewrite: Forward migration does not require rewriting historical event logs in place.

test_focus: resume from healthy materialized active state using the top-level continuation surface plus exact active transaction identity
resume from rebuild fallback when state is missing or incomplete
terminal re-entry does not resume active work
malformed or ambiguous persistence fails clearly

design_bias.preferred_anchor: Exact active transaction identity plus the top-level continuation surface (`status`, `phase`, `next_action`, `terminal`, `semantic_summary`, `verify_state`, and `commit_state`).
avoid: using planning artifacts as canonical resume truth

canonical_alignment.synchronization_intent: Use this ticket to make runtime resume behavior match the 0.6.0 exact-active-transaction protocol.
canonical_alignment.current_canonical_constraint: Implementation must preserve the single-active-transaction model and treat exact active transaction continuation as the only canonical resumable work path.
